title: sorbus aria subsp. salicifolia
intro: Sorbus aria subsp. salicifolia, also known as the Whitebeam, is a deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Rosaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicylic acid. The health benefits of Whitebeam include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The herbal preparations of Whitebeam include infusions, tinctures, and salves that can be used topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
